options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.local.type

options.description:

output.

<https://vdirsyncer.pimutils.org/en/stable/config.html#google>

options.description: The collections to synchronize between the storages.
options.type: null or (list of (string or list of string))

options.name: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.vdirsyncer.conflictResolution
options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.vdirsyncer.conflictResolution
options.description:
What to do in case of a conflict between the storages. Either
`remote wins` or
`local wins` or
a list that contains a command to run. By default, an error
message is printed.
options.type: null or one of "remote wins", "local wins" or list of string

options.name: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.vdirsyncer.enable
options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.vdirsyncer.enable
options.description: Whether to enable synchronization using vdirsyncer.

options.name: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.vdirsyncer.itemTypes
options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.vdirsyncer.itemTypes
options.description:
Kinds of items to show. The default is to show everything.
This depends on particular features of the server, the results
are not validated.

options.name: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.vdirsyncer.metadata
options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.vdirsyncer.metadata
options.description:
Metadata keys that should be synchronized when vdirsyncer
metasync is executed.

options.name: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.vdirsyncer.partialSync
options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.vdirsyncer.partialSync
options.description:
What should happen if synchronization in one direction
is impossible due to one storage being read-only.
Defaults to `revert`.

See
<https://vdirsyncer.pimutils.org/en/stable/config.html#pair-section>
for more information.

options.name: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.vdirsyncer.postHook
options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.vdirsyncer.postHook
options.description:
Command to call for each item creation and modification.
The command will be called with the path of the new/updated
file.

options.name: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.vdirsyncer.timeRange
options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.vdirsyncer.timeRange
options.description:

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.vdirsyncer.timeRange.end

options.title: accounts.calendar.accounts.<name>.vdirsyncer.urlCommand

options.description:
Sets the oauth2 params if authentication mechanism oauthbearer or
xoauth2 is used.
See {manpage}`aerc-imap(5)`.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.aerc.smtpOauth2Params.client_id
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.aerc.smtpOauth2Params.client_id

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.aerc.smtpOauth2Params.client_secret

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.aerc.smtpOauth2Params.scope

options.description: The OAuth2 requested scope.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.aerc.smtpOauth2Params.token_endpoint
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.aerc.smtpOauth2Params.token_endpoint
options.description: The OAuth2 token endpoint.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.aliases
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.aliases
options.description: Alternative identities of this account.
options.type: list of (string matching the pattern .*@.* or (submodule))

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.alot.contactCompletion
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.alot.contactCompletion
options.description:
Contact completion configuration as expected per alot.
See [alot's wiki](http://alot.readthedocs.io/en/latest/configuration/contacts_completion.html) for
explanation about possible values.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.alot.extraConfig
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.alot.extraConfig
options.description: Extra settings to add to this Alot account configuration.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.alot.sendMailCommand
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.alot.sendMailCommand
options.description:
Command to send a mail. If msmtp is enabled for the account,
then this is set to
{command}`msmtpq --read-envelope-from --read-recipients`.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.astroid.enable
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.astroid.enable
options.description: Whether to enable Astroid.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.astroid.extraConfig
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.astroid.extraConfig
options.description: Extra settings to add to this astroid account configuration.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.astroid.sendMailCommand
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.astroid.sendMailCommand
options.description:
Command to send a mail. If msmtp is enabled for the account,
then this is set to
{command}`msmtpq --read-envelope-from --read-recipients`.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.enable
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.enable
options.description:
Whether this account is enabled.  Potentially useful to allow
setting email configuration globally then enabling or disabling on
specific systems.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.flavor
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.flavor
options.description:
Some email providers have peculiar behavior that require
special treatment. This option is therefore intended to
indicate the nature of the provider.

When this indicates a specific provider then, for example,
the IMAP, SMTP, and JMAP server configuration may be set
automatically.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.folders
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.folders
options.description: Standard email folders.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.folders.drafts
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.folders.drafts
options.description: Relative path of the drafts mail folder.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.folders.inbox
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.folders.inbox
options.description: Relative path of the inbox mail.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.folders.sent
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.folders.sent
options.description: Relative path of the sent mail folder.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.folders.trash
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.folders.trash
options.description: Relative path of the deleted mail folder.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.getmail.delete
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.getmail.delete
options.description:
Enable if you want to delete read messages from the server. Most
users should either enable `delete` or disable
`readAll`.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.getmail.destinationCommand
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.getmail.destinationCommand
options.description: Specify a command delivering the incoming mail to your maildir.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.getmail.enable
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.getmail.enable
options.description: Whether to enable the getmail mail retriever for this account.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.getmail.mailboxes
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.getmail.mailboxes
options.description:
A non-empty list of mailboxes. To download all mail you can
use the `ALL` mailbox.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.getmail.readAll
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.getmail.readAll
options.description:
Enable if you want to fetch all, even the read messages from the
server. Most users should either enable `delete` or
disable `readAll`.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.gpg
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.gpg
options.description: GPG configuration.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.gpg.encryptByDefault
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.gpg.encryptByDefault
options.description: Encrypt outgoing messages by default.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.gpg.key
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.gpg.key
options.description: The key to use as listed in {command}`gpg --list-keys`.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.gpg.signByDefault
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.gpg.signByDefault
options.description: Sign messages by default.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.himalaya.enable
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.himalaya.enable
options.description: Whether to enable the email client Himalaya CLI for this email account.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.himalaya.settings
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.himalaya.settings
options.description:
Himalaya CLI configuration for this email account.
See <https://github.com/pimalaya/himalaya/blob/master/config.sample.toml> for supported values.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imap
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imap
options.description: The IMAP configuration to use for this account.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imap.authentication
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imap.authentication
options.description: The authentication mechanism.
options.type: null or string or one of "anonymous", "apop", "clear", "cram_md5", "digest_md5", "gssapi", "login", "ntlm", "plain", "xoauth2"

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imap.host
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imap.host
options.description: Hostname of IMAP server.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imap.port
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imap.port
options.description:
The port on which the IMAP server listens. If
`null` then the default port is used.
options.type: null or 16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imap.tls
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imap.tls
options.description: Configuration for secure connections.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imap.tls.certificatesFile
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imap.tls.certificatesFile
options.description:
Path to file containing certificate authorities that should
be used to validate the connection authenticity. If
`null` then the system default is used.
Note, if set then the system default may still be accepted.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imap.tls.enable
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imap.tls.enable
options.description: Whether to enable TLS/SSL.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imap.tls.useStartTls
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imap.tls.useStartTls
options.description: Whether to use STARTTLS.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imapnotify.boxes
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imapnotify.boxes
options.description: IMAP folders to watch.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imapnotify.enable
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imapnotify.enable
options.description: Whether to enable imapnotify.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imapnotify.extraArgs
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imapnotify.extraArgs
options.description: Extra arguments to pass to goimapnotify.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imapnotify.extraConfig
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imapnotify.extraConfig
options.description: Additional configuration to add for this account.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imapnotify.onNotify
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imapnotify.onNotify
options.description: Shell commands to run on any event.
options.type: string or attribute set of string

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imapnotify.onNotifyPost
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.imapnotify.onNotifyPost
options.description: Shell commands to run after onNotify event.
options.type: string or attribute set of string

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.jmap
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.jmap
options.description: The JMAP configuration to use for this account.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.jmap.host
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.jmap.host
options.description:
Hostname of JMAP server.

If both this option and [](#opt-accounts.email.accounts._name_.jmap.sessionUrl) are specified,
`host` is preferred by applications when establishing a
session.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.jmap.sessionUrl
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.jmap.sessionUrl
options.description:
URL for the JMAP Session resource.

If both this option and [](#opt-accounts.email.accounts._name_.jmap.host) are specified,
`host` is preferred by applications when establishing a
session.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.enable
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.enable
options.description: Whether to enable lieer Gmail synchronization for notmuch.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.notmuchSetupWarning
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.notmuchSetupWarning
options.description:
Warn if Notmuch is not also enabled for this account.

This can safely be disabled if {command}`notmuch init`
has been used to configure this account outside of Home
Manager.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.settings
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.settings
options.description:
Settings which are applied to {file}`.gmailieer.json`
for the account.

See the [lieer manual](https://github.com/gauteh/lieer/)
for documentation of settings not explicitly covered by this module.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.settings.drop_non_existing_label
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.settings.drop_non_existing_label
options.description: Allow missing labels on the Gmail side to be dropped.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.settings.file_extension
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.settings.file_extension
options.description:
Extension to include in local file names, which can be useful
for indexing with third-party programs.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.settings.ignore_empty_history
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.settings.ignore_empty_history
options.description:
Work around a Gmail API quirk where an empty change history
is sometimes returned.

See this
[GitHub issue](https://github.com/gauteh/lieer/issues/120)
for more details.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.settings.ignore_remote_labels
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.settings.ignore_remote_labels
options.description:
Set Gmail labels to ignore when syncing from remote labels to
local tags (before translations).

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.settings.ignore_tags
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.settings.ignore_tags
options.description:
Set labels to ignore when syncing from local tags to
remote labels (after translations).

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.settings.local_trash_tag
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.settings.local_trash_tag
options.description: Local tag to which the remote Gmail 'TRASH' label is translated.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.settings.remove_local_messages
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.settings.remove_local_messages
options.description: Remove local messages that have been deleted on the remote.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.settings.replace_slash_with_dot
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.settings.replace_slash_with_dot
options.description: Replace '/' with '.' in Gmail labels.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.settings.timeout
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.settings.timeout
options.description: HTTP timeout in seconds. 0 means forever or system timeout.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.sync.enable
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.sync.enable
options.description: Whether to enable lieer synchronization service.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.sync.frequency
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.lieer.sync.frequency
options.description:
How often to synchronize the account.

This value is passed to the systemd timer configuration as the
onCalendar option. See
{manpage}`systemd.time(7)`
for more information about the format.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.maildir
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.maildir
options.description: Maildir configuration for this account.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.maildir.path
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.maildir.path
options.description:
Path to maildir directory where mail for this account is
stored. This is relative to the base maildir path.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.create
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.create
options.description:
Automatically create missing mailboxes within the
given mail store.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.enable
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.enable
options.description: Whether to enable synchronization using mbsync.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.expunge
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.expunge
options.description:
Permanently remove messages marked for deletion from
the given mail store.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.extraConfig.account
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.extraConfig.account
options.description: Account section extra configuration.
options.type: attribute set of (string or signed integer or boolean or list of string)

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.extraConfig.channel
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.extraConfig.channel
options.description: Per channel extra configuration.
options.type: attribute set of (string or signed integer or boolean or list of string)

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.extraConfig.local
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.extraConfig.local
options.description: Local store extra configuration.
options.type: attribute set of (string or signed integer or boolean or list of string)

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.extraConfig.remote
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.extraConfig.remote
options.description: Remote store extra configuration.
options.type: attribute set of (string or signed integer or boolean or list of string)

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.flatten
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.flatten
options.description:
If set, flattens the hierarchy within the maildir by
substituting the canonical hierarchy delimiter
`/` with this value.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.groups
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.groups
options.description:
Some email providers (Gmail) have a different directory hierarchy for
synchronized email messages. Namely, when using mbsync without specifying
a set of channels into a group, all synchronized directories end up beneath
the `[Gmail]/` directory.

This option allows you to specify a group, and subsequently channels that
will allow you to sync your mail into an arbitrary hierarchy.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.groups.<name>.channels
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.groups.<name>.channels
options.description:
List of channels that should be grouped together into this group. When
performing a synchronization, the groups are synchronized, rather than
the individual channels.

Using these channels and then grouping them together allows for you to
define the maildir hierarchy as you see fit.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.groups.<name>.channels.<name>.extraConfig
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.groups.<name>.channels.<name>.extraConfig
options.description:
Extra configuration lines to add to *THIS* channel's
configuration.
options.type: attribute set of (string or signed integer or boolean or list of string)

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.groups.<name>.channels.<name>.farPattern
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.groups.<name>.channels.<name>.farPattern
options.description:
IMAP4 patterns for which mailboxes on the remote mail server to sync.
If `Patterns` are specified, `farPattern`
is interpreted as a prefix which is not matched against the patterns,
and is not affected by mailbox list overrides.

If this is left as the default, then mbsync will default to the pattern
`INBOX`.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.groups.<name>.channels.<name>.name
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.groups.<name>.channels.<name>.name
options.description:
The unique name for THIS channel in THIS group. The group will refer to
this channel by this name.

In addition, you can manually sync just this channel by specifying this
name to mbsync on the command line.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.groups.<name>.channels.<name>.nearPattern
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.groups.<name>.channels.<name>.nearPattern
options.description:
Name for where mail coming from the remote (far) mail server will end up
locally. The mailbox specified by the far pattern will be placed in
this directory.

If this is left as the default, then mbsync will default to the pattern
`INBOX`.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.groups.<name>.channels.<name>.patterns
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.groups.<name>.channels.<name>.patterns
options.description:
Instead of synchronizing *just* the mailboxes that
match the `farPattern`, use it as a prefix which is
not matched against the patterns, and is not affected by mailbox list
overrides.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.groups.<name>.name
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.groups.<name>.name
options.description:
The name of this group for this account. These names are different than
some others, because they will hide channel names that are the same.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.patterns
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.patterns
options.description: Pattern of mailboxes to synchronize.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.remove
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.remove
options.description: Propagate mailbox deletions to the given mail store.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.subFolders
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mbsync.subFolders
options.description:
The on-disk folder naming style. This option has no
effect when {option}`flatten` is used.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.meli.enable
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.meli.enable
options.description:
Whether to enable the meli mail client for this account.
Requires SMTP settings..

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.meli.mailboxAliases
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.meli.mailboxAliases
options.description: Folder display name

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.meli.mailboxes
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.meli.mailboxes
options.description: Mailboxes to show in meli

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.meli.settings
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.meli.settings
options.description: Account specific meli configuration

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.msmtp.enable
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.msmtp.enable
options.description:
Whether to enable msmtp.

If enabled then it is possible to use the
`--account` command line
option to send a message for a given account using the
{command}`msmtp` or {command}`msmtpq` tool.
For example, {command}`msmtp --account=private` would
send using the account defined in
{option}`accounts.email.accounts.private`. If the
`--account` option is not
given then the primary account will be used.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.msmtp.extraConfig
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.msmtp.extraConfig
options.description:
Extra configuration options to add to {file}`~/.msmtprc`.
See <https://marlam.de/msmtp/msmtprc.txt> for
examples.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.msmtp.tls.fingerprint
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.msmtp.tls.fingerprint
options.description:
Fingerprint of a trusted TLS certificate.
The fingerprint can be obtained by executing
{command}`msmtp --serverinfo --tls --tls-certcheck=off`.
options.type: null or string matching the pattern ([[:alnum:]]{2}:)+[[:alnum:]]{2}

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mu.enable
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mu.enable
options.description: Whether to enable mu indexing.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.enable
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.enable
options.description: Whether to enable mujmap JMAP synchronization for notmuch.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.notmuchSetupWarning
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.notmuchSetupWarning
options.description:
Warn if Notmuch is not also enabled for this account.

This can safely be disabled if {file}`mujmap.toml` is managed
outside of Home Manager.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.settings
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.settings
options.description:
Settings which are applied to {file}`mujmap.toml`
for the account.

See the [mujmap project](https://github.com/elizagamedev/mujmap)
for documentation of settings not explicitly covered by this module.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.settings.auto_create_new_mailboxes
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.settings.auto_create_new_mailboxes
options.description:
Whether to create new mailboxes automatically on the server from notmuch
tags.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.settings.cache_dir
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.settings.cache_dir
options.description:
The cache directory in which to store mail files while they are being
downloaded. The default is operating-system specific.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.settings.fqdn
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.settings.fqdn
options.description:
Fully qualified domain name of the JMAP service.

mujmap looks up the JMAP SRV record for this host to determine the JMAP session
URL. Mutually exclusive with
[](#opt-accounts.email.accounts._name_.mujmap.settings.session_url).

If `null`, defaults to
[](#opt-accounts.email.accounts._name_.jmap.host).

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.settings.password_command
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.settings.password_command
options.description:
Shell command which will print a password to stdout for basic HTTP
authentication.

If `null`, defaults to
[](#opt-accounts.email.accounts._name_.passwordCommand).
options.type: null or string or list of string

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.settings.session_url
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.settings.session_url
options.description:
Session URL to connect to.

Mutually exclusive with
[](#opt-accounts.email.accounts._name_.mujmap.settings.fqdn).

If `null`, defaults to
[](#opt-accounts.email.accounts._name_.jmap.sessionUrl).

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.settings.tags
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.settings.tags
options.description:
Tag configuration.

Beware that there are quirks that require manual consideration if changing the
values of these files; please see
<https://github.com/elizagamedev/mujmap/blob/main/mujmap.toml.example>
for more details.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.settings.tags.deleted
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.settings.tags.deleted
options.description:
Tag for notmuch to use for messages stored in the mailbox labeled with the
`Trash` name attribute.

If set to an empty string, this mailbox *and its child
mailboxes* are not synchronized with a tag.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.settings.tags.directory_separator
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.settings.tags.directory_separator
options.description: Directory separator for mapping notmuch tags to maildirs.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.settings.tags.important
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.settings.tags.important
options.description:
Tag for notmuch to use for messages stored in the mailbox labeled with the
`Important` name attribute and/or with the `$Important`
keyword.

If set to an empty string, this mailbox, *its child
mailboxes*, and these keywords are not synchronized with a tag.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.settings.tags.inbox
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.settings.tags.inbox
options.description:
Tag for notmuch to use for messages stored in the mailbox labeled with the
`Inbox` name attribute.

If set to an empty string, this mailbox *and its child
mailboxes* are not synchronized with a tag.

If set to an empty string, this mailbox *and its child
mailboxes* are not synchronized with a tag.

options.name: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.settings.tags.spam
options.title: accounts.email.accounts.<name>.mujmap.settings.tags.spam
options.description:

`Junk` name attribute and/or with the `$Junk` keyword,
*except* for messages with the `$NotJunk` keyword.

If set to an empty string, this mailbox, *its child
mailboxes*, and these keywords are not synchronized with a tag.

options.title: dbus.packages
options.description:
Packages whose D-Bus configuration files should be included in
the configuration of the D-Bus session-wide message bus. Specifically,
files in «pkg»/share/dbus-1/services will be included in the user's
$XDG_DATA_HOME/dbus-1/services directory.

options.name: dconf.databases
options.title: dconf.databases
options.description:
Settings to write to specific dconf user databases.
See [](#opt-dconf.settings) for details.
options.type: attribute set of attribute set of attribute set of (GVariant value)

options.name: dconf.settings
options.title: dconf.settings
options.description:
Settings to write to the dconf configuration system.

Note that the database is strongly-typed so you need to use the same types
as described in the GSettings schema. For example, if an option is of type
`uint32` (`u`), you need to wrap the number
using the `lib.hm.gvariant.mkUint32` constructor.
Otherwise, since Nix integers are implicitly coerced to `int32`
(`i`), it would get stored in the database as such, and GSettings
might be confused when loading the setting.

You might want to use [dconf2nix](https://github.com/gvolpe/dconf2nix)
to convert dconf database dumps into compatible Nix expression.
options.type: attribute set of attribute set of (GVariant value)

options.name: editorconfig.enable
options.title: editorconfig.enable
options.description: Whether to enable EditorConfig home configuration file.

options.name: editorconfig.settings
options.title: editorconfig.settings
options.description:

`root = true` is automatically added to the file,
it must not be added here.
See <https://editorconfig.org> for documentation.
options.type: attribute set of section of an INI file (attrs of INI atom (null, bool, int, float or string))

Per-user default emoji font(s). Multiple fonts may be listed in

Note that fontconfig matches color emoji fonts preferentially,
so if you want to use a black and white font while having
a color font installed (eg. Noto Color Emoji installed alongside

options.name: home.activation
options.title: home.activation
options.description:
The activation scripts blocks to run when activating a Home
Manager generation. Any entry here should be idempotent,
meaning running twice or more times produces the same result
as running it once.

If the script block produces any observable side effect, such
as writing or deleting files, then it
*must* be placed after the special
`writeBoundary` script block. Prior to the
write boundary one can place script blocks that verifies, but
does not modify, the state of the system and exits if an
unexpected state is found. For example, the
`checkLinkTargets` script block checks for
collisions between non-managed files and files defined in
[](#opt-home.file).

A script block should respect the {var}`DRY_RUN` variable. If it is set
then the actions taken by the script should be logged to standard out
and not actually performed. A convenient shell function {command}`run`
is provided for activation script blocks. It is used as follows:

{command}`run {command}`
: Runs the given command on live run, otherwise prints the command to
standard output.

{command}`run --quiet {command}`
: Runs the given command on live run and sends its standard output to
{file}`/dev/null`, otherwise prints the command to standard output.

{command}`run --silence {command}`
: Runs the given command on live run and sends its standard and error
output to {file}`/dev/null`, otherwise prints the command to standard
output.

The `--quiet` and `--silence` flags are mutually exclusive.

A script block should also respect the {var}`VERBOSE` variable, and if
set print information on standard out that may be useful for debugging
any issue that may arise. The variable {var}`VERBOSE_ARG` is set to
{option}`--verbose` if verbose output is enabled. You can also use the
provided shell function {command}`verboseEcho`, which acts as
{command}`echo` when verbose output is enabled.

options.name: home.checks
options.title: home.checks
options.description:
Packages that are added as dependencies of the home's build, usually
for the purpose of validating some part of the configuration.

Unlike `home.extraDependencies`, these store paths do not
become part of the built home configuration.

options.name: home.enableDebugInfo
options.title: home.enableDebugInfo
options.description:
Some Nix packages provide debug symbols for
{command}`gdb` in the `debug` output.
This option ensures that those are automatically fetched from
the binary cache if available and {command}`gdb` is
configured to find those symbols.

options.name: home.enableNixpkgsReleaseCheck
options.title: home.enableNixpkgsReleaseCheck
options.description:
Determines whether to check for release version mismatch between Home
Manager and Nixpkgs. Using mismatched versions is likely to cause errors
and unexpected behavior. It is therefore highly recommended to use a
release of Home Manager that corresponds with your chosen release of
Nixpkgs.

When this option is enabled and a mismatch is detected then a warning
will be printed when the user configuration is being built.

options.name: home.extraDependencies
options.title: home.extraDependencies
options.description:
A list of paths that should be included in the home
closure but generally not visible.

For built-time checks the `home.checks` option is more appropriate
for that purpose as checks should not leave a trace in the built
home configuration.
options.type: list of path in the Nix store

options.name: home.extraOutputsToInstall
options.title: home.extraOutputsToInstall
options.description:
List of additional package outputs of the packages
{var}`home.packages` that should be installed into
the user environment.

options.title: home.file
options.description: Attribute set of files to link into the user home.

options.title: home.file.<name>.enable
options.description:
Whether this file should be generated. This option allows specific
files to be disabled.

options.name: home.file.<name>.executable
options.title: home.file.<name>.executable
options.description:
Set the execute bit. If `null`, defaults to the mode
of the {var}`source` file or to `false`

options.name: home.file.<name>.force
options.title: home.file.<name>.force
options.description:
Whether the target path should be unconditionally replaced
by the managed file source. Warning, this will silently
delete the target regardless of whether it is a file or
link.

options.name: home.file.<name>.ignorelinks
options.title: home.file.<name>.ignorelinks
options.description:
When `recursive` is enabled, adds `-ignorelinks` flag to lndir

It causes lndir to not treat symbolic links in the source directory specially.
The link created in the target directory will point back to the corresponding
(symbolic link) file in the source directory. If the link is to a directory

options.name: home.file.<name>.onChange
options.title: home.file.<name>.onChange
options.description:
Shell commands to run when file has changed between
generations. The script will be run
*after* the new files have been linked
into place.

Note, this code is always run when `recursive` is
enabled.

options.name: home.file.<name>.recursive
options.title: home.file.<name>.recursive
options.description:
If the file source is a directory, then this option
determines whether the directory should be recursively
linked to the target location. This option has no effect
if the source is a file.

If `false` (the default) then the target
will be a symbolic link to the source directory. If
`true` then the target will be a
directory structure matching the source's but whose leafs
are symbolic links to the files of the source directory.

options.title: home.file.<name>.source

[](#opt-home.file._name_.text)

[](#opt-home.file._name_.source)
must be set.

options.name: home.pointerCursor
options.title: home.pointerCursor
options.description:
Cursor configuration.

Top-level options declared under this submodule are backend independent
options. Options declared under namespaces such as `x11`
are backend specific options. By default, only backend independent cursor
configurations are generated. If you need configurations for specific
backends, you can toggle them via the enable option. For example,
[](#opt-home.pointerCursor.x11.enable)
will enable x11 cursor configurations.

Note that this will merely generate the cursor configurations.
To apply the configurations, the relevant subsytems must also be configured.
For example, [](#opt-home.pointerCursor.gtk.enable) will generate
the gtk cursor configuration, but [](#opt-gtk.enable) needs
to be set for it to be applied.

options.description:
Extra directories to prepend to {env}`PATH`.

These directories are added to the {env}`PATH` variable in a
double-quoted context, so expressions like `$HOME` are
expanded by the shell. However, since expressions like `~` or
`*` are escaped, they will end up in the {env}`PATH`
verbatim.

options.name: home.sessionSearchVariables
options.title: home.sessionSearchVariables
options.description:
Extra directories to prepend to arbitrary PATH-like
environment variables (e.g.: {env}`MANPATH`). The values
will be concatenated by `:`.
These directories are added to the environment variable in a
double-quoted context, so expressions like `$HOME` are
expanded by the shell. However, since expressions like `~` or
`*` are escaped, they will end up in the environment
verbatim.

Individual shell integrations can be overridden with their respective

```nix

```

options.name: home.shellAliases
options.title: home.shellAliases
options.description:
An attribute set that maps aliases (the top level attribute names
in this option) to command strings or directly to build outputs.

This option should only be used to manage simple aliases that are
compatible across all shells. If you need to use a shell specific
feature then make sure to use a shell specific option, for example
[](#opt-programs.bash.shellAliases) for Bash.

options.name: home.stateVersion
options.title: home.stateVersion
options.description:
It is occasionally necessary for Home Manager to change
configuration defaults in a way that is incompatible with
stateful data. This could, for example, include switching the
default data format or location of a file.

The *state version* indicates which default
settings are in effect and will therefore help avoid breaking
program configurations. Switching to a higher state version
typically requires performing some manual steps, such as data
conversion or moving files.
